title: helianthemum guttatum subsp. littorale
intro: Helianthemum guttatum subsp. littorale, also known as rockrose, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Helianthemum guttatum subsp. littorale include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of rockrose include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and relieve skin conditions.
